inputs.reasoning: Nested repetitions trigger kInstAlt opcode handling in the switch statement, exercising the arg==0 and arg==1 cases in the alternation logic.
Multiple alternations create a complex NFA that exercises the kInstAlt case and the CheckAndLoop path with different arg values.
Large counted repetition creates many instructions, forcing the job stack to grow and triggering GrowStack() and Push() operations with njob_ >= maxjob_.
Mixed repetition operators with byte range matching exercises kInstByteRange opcode and the (p < end) condition for character consumption.
Multiple capturing groups exercise kInstCapture with arg==0 and arg==1 cases, testing capture register saving and restoration.
Non-capturing group with nested quantifiers exercises TrySearch loop and CheckAndLoop goto path, testing the while(njob_ > 0) condition.
Negated character class exercises byte range matching with complex character set logic in kInstByteRange.
Very large upper bound on counted repetition forces significant job stack growth, testing the reallocation and push logic.
Alternation within quantifiers exercises kInstAlt with greedy/non-greedy matching and kInstAltMatch opcode handling.
Multiple sequential quantifiers create complex NFA requiring extensive job stack manipulation and ShouldVisit checks in the main loop.
